clean_body: We have the formula to win this seat and take back Texas in 2022. Now we just need you! | = It's official, friend. Redistricting in Texas is complete and Austin -- for the first time in decades -- got a brand new district. This is a potential pickup for Democrats in Texas. This week, I announced my run for this brand new seat, Texas' 37th Congressional District. In a moment I'm going to ask you to = to be a Founding Donor to my campaign. But first, let me tell you why I'm running. I've lived in Austin for over 15 years and built a career in computer engineering at some of Austin's most iconic companies from Motorola/Freescale to Dell. During this time, Austin grew to be the 11th largest city in the country. As an active volunteer in my community, I served as president of a non-profit with 4,000+ members that provides free training and education. We've helped thousands of people, but I realized direct service alone isn't enough. We needed to do more -- and that's why I'm running. This year we witnessed the GOP restrict voting rights and further gerrymander our state to weaken the voices of Texans -- despite the fact that Hispanic, Black, and Asian Americans accounted for 95% of Texas's population growth in the last decade. We can change this and take back Texas. Here's how: In 2018, Beto O'Rourke was just short of winning a US Senate seat by 214,921 votes. In 2022 it's up to all candidates to work together and close that gap. We're expanding the electorate by building a coalition of all Texans -- Democrats, independents, and moderates. That's how we win and take back Texas. In 2020, my campaign got the highest Democratic voter turnout in the history of the district. We went to over 50,000 doors in Williamson County, making it #1 in turnout among the top 25 most populated counties in Texas. That's why we're in the best position to repeat our success in Austin and help Democrats win statewide. With our election in just a few months, I need to meet every fundraising mark with strength -- especially since we're going up against the Texas GOP. I need to know you're with me. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Congress needs leadership in science and technology to advance the next generation of innovation. I'll use my expertise in engineering to deliver solutions that work for all of us. With your support we can win this seat and take back Texas! Thanks in advance, Donna = | = Donna Imam is a computer engineer and Democrat running for US Congress in Austin, Texas. If you'd like to learn more, you can visit Donna's website Consider supporting Donna's campaign by = If you'd prefer to send a check, our address is: PO BOX 170305 Austin, TX 78717. Please make the check payable to Donna Imam for Congress. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Donna Imam For Congress PO BOX 170305 Austin, TX 78717 United States

name: ICYMI News
subject: Reason with precision
body:
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Eucli
| _Sponsored message_ |
---|---|---
This sponsored message from ICYMI News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zcv9v0pe5tm0dskg4302ssu4h72v5o33g1dap9do_rp22sh2s8i66pj0sdj6cor6c1h2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid.
While studying law in Springfield, Lincoln explained, he became convinced that the ability to demonstrate the truth of things was essential to the good practice of law.
Consequently, Lincoln left his job, retired to his father’s cabin, and did not return until he could “give any proposition in the six books of Euclid at sight.”
By studying Euclid, we improve our ability to reason soundly—plus, in my experience, the study of Euclid can be great fun.
That’s why Hillsdale College has produced a free online course on the topic **“Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry.”**
Through**“Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry,”** you will learn the importance of deductive reasoning as a foundation for pursuing knowledge in all fields of learning, as well as in everyday life.
---
| | **Now, the course is available in a beautiful DVD box set for viewing in your home or for use in a small group.** **** **You can receive the DVD box set as a thank-you for your tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty.**
Your tax-deductible gift will help promote our free online courses and other educational outreach efforts in this critical time for our nation. And your gift matters even more because...
Hillsdale accomplishes all its work while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.
---
| [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zupsp7ms5bd4s91uq9a5828o43f07k7ijcp85eeo_rp22sh2s8i66pj0sdj6cor6c1h2bs)
---
| | That’s why we need the support of friends like you, who understand the importance of education to liberty.
Quantities of this special DVD box set are limited, so reserve your copy today. I’ve included a secure link for you to make your tax-deductible gift below:
[_**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/dvd-mathematics-and-logic-course**_](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zgapo6p52fo78481r76fjfpjl1072bhle31l66sg_rp22sh2s8i66pj0sdj6cor6c1h2bs)
Thank you for partnering with Hillsdale in its mission on behalf of liberty.
Warm regards,
Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---
[**Visit ICYMI News**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zilqkqgt5mk3u4fm1vneh3mvir7adafievb6rbpo_rp22sh2s8i66pj0sdj6cor6c1h2bs)

625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

[Click here to change your subscription preferences](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z92p9260au4guu7ihlv3r37r0njvu3hoofso5ko0_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9jle8r7ccjkm1on9dtjmppq9dhkumqb7d9neppqcdtlmuju1df34kqu2c3neior3d5medjm05plmgqb3ct66uqr69bls2on2s3isujqact4moqfbd5jmkrncst66uqrf8pnm9qrf9bjmpqn4djm69qb0sbkmuprf972u9hf8d34e9gb7d77c1h31p36eer61c32cmpr6dtlc5gj4clgmsrrbcrje8qrat3ge1h238h1e8he4933cpge6pj6cdj60oh5u)

name: Ras Smith
subject: Why Iowa needs a Democrat.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ras Smith for Governor
body:
You probably remember what happened last time Iowa received aid of this magnitude from the federal government (hint: it wasn't good!) |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/39340255/319394445/692104027?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzkzZWEwMS0xZjU1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0wMDUwZjJlNjVlOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XqnbyibTMtYDCfykfuu6wuWA9qEabseTcJu-BW-hMto=)
Team,

Last month, Democrats in Washington passed a $1.2 trillion infrastructure package with bipartisan support. It rebuilds roads, bridges, mass transit, and waterways, and expands broadband access.

The initiative is expected to create hundreds of thousands of family-sustaining jobs. Projects are expected to start as early as next year. **Iowa is set to receive $5 billion in federal funding.**

You probably remember what happened last time Iowa received aid of this magnitude from the federal government (hint: it wasn't good):
| **"Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds defends returning $95 million from feds for COVID testing in schools"

"Audit: Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds improperly used COVID funds for salaries"**
---

We can't risk any more waste, fraud, and abuse from the governor when it comes to distributing and utilizing these funds. **Iowans need a champion for working families, not someone who will turn around and use federal funds for their own staff salaries.**

Look friend, you have my word that when I'm elected governor, I'll put Iowa first. I'll put working families first. I'll put our farmers, small businesses owners, and importantly, our kids first.

But in order to build the biggest grassroots operation possible so that we can flip Iowa blue, I need a ton of people chipping in small amounts to help us meet our upcoming end-of-year deadline. It's a BIG one, that's why I'm asking:

[**Will you chip in $3 -- or whatever you can -- to help me get a good start on the month of December? Anything you give goes toward reaching every Democrat and undecided voter in every corner of this state.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/39340260/319394446/-73891749?refcode=em-12421-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzkzZWEwMS0xZjU1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0wMDUwZjJlNjVlOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XqnbyibTMtYDCfykfuu6wuWA9qEabseTcJu-BW-hMto=)

Thanks in advance for anything you can do.

Ras Smith
Democrat for Governor

clean_body: You probably remember what happened last time Iowa received aid of this magnitude from the federal government (hint: it wasn't good!) | = Team, Last month, Democrats in Washington passed a $1.2 trillion infrastructure package with bipartisan support. It rebuilds roads, bridges, mass transit, and waterways, and expands broadband access. The initiative is expected to create hundreds of thousands of family-sustaining jobs. Projects are expected to start as early as next year. Iowa is set to receive $5 billion in federal funding. You probably remember what happened last time Iowa received aid of this magnitude from the federal government (hint: it wasn't good): | "Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds defends returning $95 million from feds for COVID testing in schools" "Audit: Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds improperly used COVID funds for salaries" We can't risk any more waste, fraud, and abuse from the governor when it comes to distributing and utilizing these funds. Iowans need a champion for working families, not someone who will turn around and use federal funds for their own staff salaries. Look friend, you have my word that when I'm elected governor, I'll put Iowa first. I'll put working families first. I'll put our farmers, small businesses owners, and importantly, our kids first. But in order to build the biggest grassroots operation possible so that we can flip Iowa blue, I need a ton of people chipping in small amounts to help us meet our upcoming end-of-year deadline. It's a BIG one, that's why I'm asking: = Thanks in advance for anything you can do. Team, When I first began serving Colorado, I was called the “accidental senator.” But after putting my head down, doing the work, and securing wins for the people of Colorado, winning re-election was no accident. But it was only possible because of you. This team pitched in to build the infrastructure required to win. You might be surprised to hear this, but a large portion of our budget comes from grassroots contributions to emails just like this one. **And, I’m the only Democratic Senator running for re-election that’s refusing to accept a single cent of corporate PAC and federal lobbyist money because I believe fighting to undo the influence of money in politics means leading by example.** From day one, this team has been fueled by hard-working folks chipping in what they could for a movement we all believe in. There will never be a question of who I work for — that answer will always be you. But to win this race and keep Colorado blue, our campaign has to reach and mobilize voters across the state while battling attacks from Mitch McConnell and dark money groups. It’s expensive work with the National GOP raking in millions and wealthy self-funded opponents launching attack ads against me, we can’t rest for a moment. **So will you pitch in just $10 today? name: Senate Conservatives
subject: Coyote-in-Chief!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SENATE CONSERVATIVES FUND AND NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
| |
---
Peter:

It's true. The U.S. government really did charter secret flights to relocate illegal immigrants into our country in the middle of the night.

Just as the chaos in southern Texas **IS** Joe Biden's border policy, smuggling illegals into the country **IS** his immigration policy.
At this point, Biden is nothing more than **Coyote-in-Chief**.

His Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas finally admitted what everyone knew: the illegal immigrants Biden is letting into the country are bringing Covid-19 with them.

And his Vice President, Kamala Harris, who is supposedly _**IN CHARGE**_ of getting the border under control, is too busy auditioning child actors for her TV ads to bother.

Nor have Democrats in Congress lifted a finger to secure the border. They think the border is racist. They think immigration enforcement is racist. They think America is racist!

**[ Take a stand and help stop the Coyote-in-Chief! ](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58936018:JQQC4NEvN:m:1:3298722022:FFD6F96D311DE1BB28D8B34DD14FB561:r) **

Democrats are still in charge of our government for at least another year and a half. And they won't give up their anti-American bigotry unless we give them the political defeat of a lifetime next year.

** But we all know, it's not enough to turn blue Senate seats red. We need true conservative fighters like: **

✅ **Mo Brooks** in Alabama
✅ **Josh Mandel** in Ohio
✅ **Mike Lee** in Utah
✅ **Ron Johnson** in Wisconsin
✅ **Ted Budd** in North Carolina
✅ **Rand Paul** in Kentucky, and
✅ **Adam Laxalt** in Nevada.

**[ We need them ALL in the Senate! ](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58936018:JQQC4NEvN:m:1:3298722022:FFD6F96D311DE1BB28D8B34DD14FB561:r) **

They are not just Republicans and conservatives. They are patriots, ready to fight and sacrifice to rescue America from the woke, socialist fanatics leading us into inflation, crime, censorship, election corruption, defeat, and racist indoctrination.

Senate Conservatives Fund has already done so much to impact these Senate races and, with your help, we're going to win back the Senate not for a party, but _**for our nation**_.

**[ Please join us by making a split contribution to our endorsed Senate candidates today! ](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58936018:JQQC4NEvN:m:1:3298722022:FFD6F96D311DE1BB28D8B34DD14FB561:r) **

Together, we can build an earthquake election that fills the Senate with true conservatives who will fight for our future.

Thank you for considering this important request.

Sincerely,
Mary Vought
Executive Director
Senate Conservatives Fund
|

body:
If we want a government that works for working people, we must have more working people in government
#

Friend,
Politics isn't a TV show — and policy isn't a game. Their impacts are real, and their outcomes are deadly serious.
That's why Pennsylvania absolutely cannot afford an ultra-wealthy, reality TV senator like Dr. Oz.
Far too few of our elected officials know in their bones what working families like yours and mine are going through.
So the solution is clear: If we want a government that works for working people, we must have more working people in government.
**If I have the honor of serving as Pennsylvania's next U.S. Senator, I'll fight like hell to make sure our needs are met, our voices are heard, and our people are present everywhere decisions about our lives are being made.**
[But I need you with me. name: Aadland For Senate
subject: Reminder: Erik answers a critic
body:
Uniting Americans does not mean a move to the left
---
| | | | | | Volume 17 | November 17, 2021
---
|
---
| A Word From Erik
---
| | |
---|---
|
On Tuesday, Nov. 9, I spoke at the Arapahoe County Tea Party meeting. There I shared about bringing our country together.

One attendee was critical of my position, saying that the only way to come together is to move to the Left.

"Republicans have been trying to do this for 70 years and we're on the verge of losing the country," he wrote in an email. "Your position merely makes you a new RINO. It's a losing position in this environment." As a war veteran, he said their strategy was not to "reach out" to the enemy.

Our culture is filled with so much anger and vitriol. But what people want is integrity and truth. I believe in addressing criticism as it comes, so I share with you my response:

"Thanks for your message. As always, there's room for either miscommunication or misinterpretation; based on your message below, one or the other occurred. I accept full responsibility for what I said and any lack of clarity in my presentation I own fully--I'm not pawning it off on a consultant or blaming it on bad advice. I told you exactly what I think, though I can always improve my clarity or communication skills.
I spoke at length about defending the Constitution and why we're on the verge of losing our country. Never did I suggest we compromise our principles or bend to the Left.
What I did say was that the 'enemy' is not the average Liberal or Democrat down the street, but corrupt individuals in our government and institutions (Democrat and Republican). This is an American problem, not a problem of Right vs. Left.
I am proposing a political way to restore this country and our government back to 'We the People' by focusing on the real enemy (the DC swamp, corrupt politicians, MSM, certain corporations, etc.) and uniting on core principles. Civil war and violence are not the answer, nor will it serve us or our children in the long run. In fact, many foreign and domestic enemies would take swift advantage of such a situation. We both know the horrors of war--for me that is the last resort.
We certainly don't need more hatred, division, or polarization along superficial lines--what we need is Americans to band together to take their power back. This will require leadership and an unprecedented level of activation.
I've never suggested we reach across the aisle to work with corrupt politicians or self-avowed Socialists or Communists in our government. I'm talking about bridging the gap dividing the American people.
We must come together as Americans now or we lose this country. Those who think cleaning out the corruption and restoring this government back to 'We the People' can be done only by conservatives or Republicans (28% of voters in CO) are wrong--or perhaps they just want a hill to die on. The unaffiliated are 43% of Colorado voters and we need to enroll them in this cause to win statewide office.
Yes, I'm talking about unifying because it must be done to save this country. I'm in this to save my country, not to become a U.S. Senator, or to accumulate power, prestige, or wealth; I'm doing this for my children's future.
I'm not a politician; nobody is telling me what to say. I speak from the heart, and I've thought endlessly about how to save our country peacefully. I tell people the hard truth of how to take back this country politically, and what must be done to win in Colorado. Better we act from a position of power than defeat and weakness.
Furthermore, I'm a follower of Jesus Christ who taught us to love even our enemies. Honestly, I don't have time for those who want to continue hating and fighting with their neighbors--it's a losing proposition based on fear, powerlessness, and lack of faith.
All things can be done through Faith by following the principles of Christ. That's what I aim to do while honoring my oath to support and defend the Constitution against enemies foreign and domestic."
In Service,
|
---

On Friday, Nov. 19, I met with some great people concerned about liberty in Westminster. Then on Saturday, Nov. 20 I traveled once again to Colorado Springs.

The thing for which I am most grateful is my family, so the Thanksgiving holiday was spent with them enjoying the many blessings bestowed upon us by our Creator.

Next week I will be in full swing traveling to communities throughout the Front Range and will appear on the Mandy Connell Show.

I sincerely hope to see you soon on the trail!

clean_body: Uniting Americans does not mean a move to the left | | | | | | Volume 17 | November 17, 2021 | | A Word From Erik | | | ---|--- | On Tuesday, Nov. 9, I spoke at the Arapahoe County Tea Party meeting. There I shared about bringing our country together. One attendee was critical of my position, saying that the only way to come together is to move to the Left. "Republicans have been trying to do this for 70 years and we're on the verge of losing the country," he wrote in an email. "Your position merely makes you a new RINO. It's a losing position in this environment." As a war veteran, he said their strategy was not to "reach out" to the enemy. Our culture is filled with so much anger and vitriol. But what people want is integrity and truth. I believe in addressing criticism as it comes, so I share with you my response: "Thanks for your message. As always, there's room for either miscommunication or misinterpretation; based on your message below, one or the other occurred. I accept full responsibility for what I said and any lack of clarity in my presentation I own fully--I'm not pawning it off on a consultant or blaming it on bad advice. I told you exactly what I think, though I can always improve my clarity or communication skills. I spoke at length about defending the Constitution and why we're on the verge of losing our country. Never did I suggest we compromise our principles or bend to the Left. What I did say was that the 'enemy' is not the average Liberal or Democrat down the street, but corrupt individuals in our government and institutions (Democrat and Republican). This is an American problem, not a problem of Right vs. Left. I am proposing a political way to restore this country and our government back to 'We the People' by focusing on the real enemy (the DC swamp, corrupt politicians, MSM, certain corporations, etc.) and uniting on core principles. Civil war and violence are not the answer, nor will it serve us or our children in the long run. In fact, many foreign and domestic enemies would take swift advantage of such a situation. We both know the horrors of war--for me that is the last resort. We certainly don't need more hatred, division, or polarization along superficial lines--what we need is Americans to band together to take their power back. This will require leadership and an unprecedented level of activation. I've never suggested we reach across the aisle to work with corrupt politicians or self-avowed Socialists or Communists in our government. I'm talking about bridging the gap dividing the American people. We must come together as Americans now or we lose this country. Those who think cleaning out the corruption and restoring this government back to 'We the People' can be done only by conservatives or Republicans (28% of voters in CO) are wrong--or perhaps they just want a hill to die on. The unaffiliated are 43% of Colorado voters and we need to enroll them in this cause to win statewide office. Yes, I'm talking about unifying because it must be done to save this country. I'm in this to save my country, not to become a U.S. Senator, or to accumulate power, prestige, or wealth; I'm doing this for my children's future. I'm not a politician; nobody is telling me what to say. I speak from the heart, and I've thought endlessly about how to save our country peacefully. I tell people the hard truth of how to take back this country politically, and what must be done to win in Colorado. Better we act from a position of power than defeat and weakness. Furthermore, I'm a follower of Jesus Christ who taught us to love even our enemies. Honestly, I don't have time for those who want to continue hating and fighting with their neighbors--it's a losing proposition based on fear, powerlessness, and lack of faith. All things can be done through Faith by following the principles of Christ. That's what I aim to do while honoring my oath to support and defend the Constitution against enemies foreign and domestic." ---

Here are five things you need to know about Huwaida Arraf:
1. She founded an anti-Israel group _**known for provoking violent confrontations**_ with the Israeli military that _**has been investigated by the FBI for possible ties to terrorism.**_
2. She co-authored an op-ed that promoted violence against Israelis and said: “ _**Yes, people will get killed and injured,**_ ” but these deaths are “no less noble than carrying out a suicide operation. And we are certain that if these men were killed during such an action, _**they would be considered shaheed Allah.**_ ”
3. She has _**provided protection for terrorists,**_ even acting as a voluntary human shield for them.
4. She co-organized the “Free Gaza Flotilla” _**which led to violent clashes**_ with the Israeli Navy.
5. Now, _**she’s running for Congress**_ in Michigan’s 10th Congressional District.

Nothing is more important than stopping Huwaida Arraf.

In order to do that, we must support Rep. Lisa McClain – the pro-Israel Republican who is currently representing the district.

---

**[If you stand with Israel, I am urging you to rush anything you can to help Rep. Lisa McClain defeat Huwaida Arraf.](https://trk.cp20.com/click/gh7s-301xh-1nrs1-4qqlpo4/ "If you stand with Israel, I am urging you to rush anything you can to help Rep. Lisa McClain defeat Huwaida Arraf.")**

Thank you in advance for your generous support.

My very best,

Nikki R. Haley

name: Geoff Diehl
subject: Reminder: Response to Baker/Polito Announcement Today
body:
You don't want to miss this.
| | | |
---
---
---
| I thank Governor Baker and Lt. Gov. Polito for their eight years of service as Governor and Lt. Gov. of Massachusetts. While I have often differed on policy with this administration, I have always liked Charlie Baker and Karyn Polito personally and I wish them the best in their future endeavors.
I am honored and gratified to have been endorsed by former President Donald Trump to be the next Governor of Massachusetts, and I look forward to a spirited campaign presenting my vision for the future of Massachusetts. My campaign is providing Bay Staters with a hopeful choice for fresh leadership and a bold new direction for our state.
I have a message to Massachusetts residents who are tired of the status quo: I will work hard every day to make our state a better place to live, work, and raise a family. I believe that starts by placing more emphasis on individual freedom and initiative. I will fight for people who need help. I'll support struggling working families. I will oppose new taxes. I will support parents who don't want government taking away their freedom to make choices about their child's health care and curriculum. And, I'll always have the back of first responders working to keep our streets safe, as well as the men and women of the armed forces who hail from Massachusetts and serve overseas protecting our freedoms.
We can do better for Massachusetts. Over the next year, the Diehl campaign will discuss the important issues facing our state and present the best alternative for leading Massachusetts into the future.

---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
|
---

name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: The Anti-CRT Curriculum
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DAN CRENSHAW FOR CONGRESS
body:
This week: Marxist ideology in classrooms, Mayor Pete's out of touch again, and prosecutors letting dangerous criminals out of jail
---
| | | | | | |
---
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001VbLwE1U6Ht_lL7Y8jpEzvuO0rD54tsHHCq-bIV-CYcsqxKULDq-hN-s2S4yTlGodKy9cyWAwTh1H4bUsYyl3GmznUU51so3hJRkoqYnKahAcD-mPPahqzIUBe98mEebK3jQ6UvZiu72MjuAtWzZIlMLY1fEDWkhHCCGiTC2Qab_DloGwl-tOyGKls9M9Az4S2jssL1HwgMKWcnwLgVEN-5dp_h0hXS_gGD0jbZiLPHtVsm1ijRbmLmMmGsiGC_Gtk94kxCJsVaQCVOayxHHMYAD-2RZYaasiImEFz2fQxx-7qIWZJ6HaeQ8SHmSI7PDT&c=XhZ2e2IYq0twyHOZhc_4Ko3MqIgM5y_N0FBVkke_Sm0GM3mmzdw65Q==&ch=NpBnFhD9RDPAJDi6R2DGjr9kKm0AhfVC0cj8yol0VXZBcN2-yGbTPA==)
---
|
Peter-
Dan Crenshaw here.
This week, I introduced legislation in the House to combat Critical Race Theory by offering America's schools an alternative: teach our kids to love America and hate communism, instead of the other way around. I also criticized Pete Buttigieg for claiming Americans don't have to worry about gas prices, and criticized liberal policies that lead to violent crimes like the vehicle attack in Wisconsin.

clean_body: This week: Marxist ideology in classrooms, Mayor Pete's out of touch again, and prosecutors letting dangerous criminals out of jail | | | | | | | | == | Peter- Dan Crenshaw here. This week, I introduced legislation in the House to combat Critical Race Theory by offering America's schools an alternative: teach our kids to love America and hate communism, instead of the other way around. I also criticized Pete Buttigieg for claiming Americans don't have to worry about gas prices, and criticized liberal policies that lead to violent crimes like the vehicle attack in Wisconsin. As always, thank you for your support and please stay up to date with me on ==, ==, and ==. Now, here's your brief. | | | | | | THE ANTI-CRT CURRICULUM | == | == | The Soviet Union put vast amounts of resources and time into spreading communist ideology around the world. They used the KGB to infiltrate American society to spread this dangerous and destructive ideology right here in our country, using useful idiots that lead key American institutions. From the Marxist-infused radicalism of the '60s to the Critical Race Theory trends of today, I don't think the Soviets could have imagined a better ally than the socialists in modern America, some of whom currently serve in our Congress. == That's why I introduced legislation called the Crucial Communism Teaching Act to stop Critical Race Theory and Marxism from being taught in America's schools. And while the left lies that CRT is not being taught, we know that isn't true. And we know CRT is an ideology that must be stopped. We need to provide American schools, and more importantly American parents, an alternative to the Marxist ideology currently being taught to our children. Parents deserve to have a say in what their children learn in school, and I believe parents would rather have their children learn to love America and hate communism than the other way around. That's what this bill does. It ensures students know the lessons of history so that our next generation does not repeat them. It will ensure communism remains in the trash heap of history, where it belongs. | | | == | | | | | | | | MAYOR PETE IS OUT OF TOUCH | == | == | Pete Buttigieg is an awful cabinet Secretary. Pete went on vacation for months while America was experiencing the worst supply chain crisis in history. Pete was promoting his own documentary while grocery store shelves sat empty. And now, what is Pete's message to Americans worried about record high gas prices? Don't worry about it, get an electric vehicle and stop polluting. Imagine being so out of touch that you think spending tens of thousands more on an electric car is easy for most people. 78% of EV subsidies go to those making six figures. And the rest of us still worry about gas prices, because gas affects the cost of literally everything. There's no way to escape this reality: Joe Biden's policies promoted by people like Mayor Pete have caused the worst energy crisis we've seen in years. And instead of taking action to lower gas prices, they decide to promote their anti-science Green New Deal agenda. You can't make this stuff up. | | | == | | | | | | | | SOME PROSECUTORS WANT CRIMINALS OUT OF JAIL | == | == | "Is there going to be an individual I divert, or I put into treatment program, who's going to go out and kill somebody? You bet. Guaranteed. It's guaranteed to happen." That's an actual quote from the liberal District Attorney for Milwaukee, Wisconsin, where six people are dead and dozens more injured after a repeat violent felon drove his car into a Christmas parade. The policy that the district attorney is referring to is the policy of letting violent offenders out on bail, even after they attempt to kill people. The murderer who killed those parade goers in Wisconsin was arrested just a couple weeks before for attempting to run over the mother of his child. He was released on $1,000 bond. These district attorneys are bank rolled by liberal billionaires like George Soros, who personally dumped millions into local races to elect woke prosecutors who would push for bail reform. What we saw in Wisconsin is just the most recent example of the consequences. This is why local elections matter. Don't let people like George Soros dictate who is in charge of keeping your community safe. Get involved, get informed, and elect local officials who will enforce the law and keep violent criminals off your streets. | | | | | | | | ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | | | | | | RECOMMENDED READS DAILY WIRE: == FOX: == NATIONAL REVIEW: == Thank you for reading! In Service, | | Dan Crenshaw | | | | | == | | | Thank you for fighting for our conservative values. Your support is extremely valuable and crucial for Republicans to take back the House and Senate in 2022. Dan Crenshaw is counting on you to help him restore America and teach our kids to to be grateful for our Founding, our Constitution, our Flag, and our People. name: Cora Neumann
subject: "Show up everywhere"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cora for Montana
body:
| Friend,

I can hardly believe we're already in the final month of 2021.

It's a bit of a blur looking back on this past year on the trail. All the cities and towns we visited. The Democratic county meetings and lunches. But what stands out most are the face-to-face conversations I've had. It's been humbling to get to know so many new friends -- _and I look forward to making many more!_

I’ve always loved the road, road-trips are part of life in Montana. And In just the last month I've been up to Lincoln County to visit folks in Libby. I've made stops in the Flathead, Sanders County, Kalispell, and Deer Lodge. And that's just to name a few...

It's _a lot_ of ground to cover, but that's what it’s going to take to run the type of campaign we’re committed to -- a campaign that gives voice to Montanans everywhere. No communities, no matter how remote or small they may be, will be ignored.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/39343158/319404765/102973118?refcode=em-12.4.21-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODQ0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNzVhYTg4OC0yYTU1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0wMDUwZjJlNjVlOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ty5qFW43Xm7iLyLHMsQvgyrSd7sEXS-bTvmsw8-tYPI=)
_We always find moments to take in Montana's beauty. Here’s a photo I took from the road. The Cabinet Mountains in Sanders County._
I say this a lot, but I truly can’t thank you enough for your support. Your donations put gas in the car so I can drive across this massive congressional district. And soon, your dollars will fill the tanks of on-the-ground organizers who will knock doors across our state.

**[Before we close out 2021, we set our most ambitious online fundraising goal yet: $35,000 in grassroots donations to run the most inclusive campaign possible. If you have the means, please chip in $3 today to help us get a head start on this goal.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/39343159/319404766/102973118?refcode=em-12.4.21-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODQ0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNzVhYTg4OC0yYTU1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0wMDUwZjJlNjVlOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ty5qFW43Xm7iLyLHMsQvgyrSd7sEXS-bTvmsw8-tYPI=)**

If our campaign had a motto, it'd be "show up everywhere." I'm ready to do just that on the trail and in Congress. But I can't do this alone, and truthfully, I wouldn't want to.

So thanks in advance for your support,

Cora _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/39343160/319404767/102973118?refcode=em-12.4.21-all-b&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODQ0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNzVhYTg4OC0yYTU1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0wMDUwZjJlNjVlOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ty5qFW43Xm7iLyLHMsQvgyrSd7sEXS-bTvmsw8-tYPI=)

clean_body: | Friend, I can hardly believe we're already in the final month of 2021. It's a bit of a blur looking back on this past year on the trail. All the cities and towns we visited. The Democratic county meetings and lunches. But what stands out most are the face-to-face conversations I've had. It's been humbling to get to know so many new friends -- and I look forward to making many more! I’ve always loved the road, road-trips are part of life in Montana. And In just the last month I've been up to Lincoln County to visit folks in Libby. I've made stops in the Flathead, Sanders County, Kalispell, and Deer Lodge. And that's just to name a few... It's a lot of ground to cover, but that's what it’s going to take to run the type of campaign we’re committed to -- a campaign that gives voice to Montanans everywhere. No communities, no matter how remote or small they may be, will be ignored. = We always find moments to take in Montana's beauty. Here’s a photo I took from the road. The Cabinet Mountains in Sanders County. I say this a lot, but I truly can’t thank you enough for your support. Your donations put gas in the car so I can drive across this massive congressional district. And soon, your dollars will fill the tanks of on-the-ground organizers who will knock doors across our state. = If our campaign had a motto, it'd be "show up everywhere." I'm ready to do just that on the trail and in Congress. But I can't do this alone, and truthfully, I wouldn't want to. So thanks in advance for your support, Cora If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Paid for by Cora for Montana Cora Neumann is a public health and economic development expert running for Congress in Montana's new 2nd Congressional District. To learn more about Cora, visit her website Consider supporting Cora's campaign = We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Cora for Montana PO Box 428 Bozeman, MT 59771 United States

In case y’all missed it — This week, 26 (!!) Texas Democratic Party officers across the state endorsed Mike to be the 2022 Democratic nominee for Texas Lt. Governor. This is — and we cannot emphasize it enough — a _huge_ deal! Let us explain: In Texas, one of the primary roles of the lieutenant governor is to work with the Texas Senate to tackle the pressing issues we face. So the fact that 26 elected leaders from the Texas Senate Democratic Executive Committee — representing __ every region of the state (check out the map!) — endorsed Mike is a _serious_ testament to the movement we’re building together. clean_body: | In case y’all missed it — This week, 26 (!!) Texas Democratic Party officers across the state endorsed Mike to be the 2022 Democratic nominee for Texas Lt. Governor. This is — and we cannot emphasize it enough — a huge deal! Let us explain: In Texas, one of the primary roles of the lieutenant governor is to work with the Texas Senate to tackle the pressing issues we face. So the fact that 26 elected leaders from the Texas Senate Democratic Executive Committee — representing every region of the state (check out the map!) — endorsed Mike is a serious testament to the movement we’re building together. = = Now more than ever, Texans deserve a lieutenant governor who will lead with compassion and fight for a future that represents all of us. As the elected leaders of our party, our endorsers understand what's at stake in 2022 and know that Mike is the best candidate to take on Dan Patrick — so much so that exactly one week from now, Mike will be standing alongside this coalition while he officially files for the 2022 ballot. (We’re so proud!) = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Folks, it’s obvious, Texas is lining up behind Mike — and for good reason. Now, let’s do everything we can to keep the momentum going and make sure Mike has the support he needs to come out on top. Thank you so much for your continued support, Team Collier The Collier Committee P.O. Box 14648 Humble, TX 77347 United States | | Paid for by the Collier Committee

name: Patrick Clark, Keystone Progress
subject: Dr. Oz, Pennsylvania needs you to sit down.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www
body:
Dear Friend,

We have a message for Dr. Oz, the TV doctor who doles out dubious medical advice: Go back to Oz!

But wait. Where is he even from? Whether he’s from New Jersey or Australia, he’s definitely not a person who represents Pennsylvanians -- not our lives, our work, or our values.

And yet, Dr. Oz announced a Senate run to represent PA and replace retiring Sen. Toomey. His fellow Republicans seem pretty pleased with it, including Trump’s saddest shadow, Sen. Lindsey Graham, who called it “good news for the Republican Party.” It's clear that Dr. Oz is likewise salivating for Trump's approval.

Dr. Oz is not a politician, he’s a talk show host who, despite a medical degree, has some backwards views on protecting people from COVID-19. He's against life-saving vaccine mandates and promoted the use of hydroxychloroquine to treat the virus. Those aren't the views of any doctor we'd want to consult.

It’s still early days, but it’s not too early for us to know that Dr. Oz is not the person for this job. We need to make sure that this guy does not get elected.

Help support our work to mobilize voters across the state to vote for progressive candidates who aren’t doctors speaking out against science or hawking fake weight-loss medication.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=120421 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=120421]

Let's keep pushing for a progressive PA!

Patrick Clark
Keystone Progress

https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/ [https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/]

Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www.kpef.info. Not affiliated with any candidate or candidate's committees.

Keystone Progress
354 S Atlantic Ave
Pittsburgh, PA 15224

name: =?utf-8?Q?Douglas=20Carswell?=
subject: Supreme Court, Dobbs, and State Autonomy – update from the Mississippi Center for Public Policy
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/progressupdate-8132154?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| Dear Friend,

In 2018, Mississippi passed a law restricting abortion procedures after the first 15 weeks of pregnancy. This law, which the Mississippi Center for Public Policy played a key role in preparing, has never been applied.

A number of courts have ruled that the law violates women’s rights as established in _Roe v. Wade_ in 1973.

This week, however, the case – _Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization_ – finally reached the Supreme Court, which must now decide if the 15-week restriction is indeed constitutional.

It could be that the Supreme Court upholds the decisions by the lower courts, and nothing changes. **But it might just be that we see a really quite subtle and important change in America’s political process that goes far beyond the issue of abortion.**
---
|
---
| If the Supreme Court were to rule in favor of Mississippi’s law, women across America would continue to have less restricted access to abortion than women have in most European states. Claims that the Supreme Court ruling would ban abortion strike me as far-fetched.

What _Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization _might just do is not just overturn _Roe v. Wade_ as much as upend the assumption that it is for authorities at the federal level to decide what the law ought to be uniformly on this issue.

**If Mississippi was free to do things a little differently, might not Massachusetts or Maryland, or Michigan follow suit? **And in what direction might they choose to diverge? And if states, not federal institutions, could do some things differently, why not others?

The effect of taking power away from judges in DC to decide, and giving it to those elected in each state would be profound.

**Judicial activism has reduced a lot of America’s national politics to a process of trying to pack Courts and skew judicial nominations.** If the Supreme Court rules that it should really be up to elected officials at the state level to decide more of the policy, politics might once again become an exercise in persuasion.

I suspect America would end up with better public policy overall. Watch this case with interest.

**It would be a remarkable achievement for a state-based organization such as ours if the law we helped author in 2018 also ended up helping shift the balance of power away from the federal machine and back to the states. **

Have a wonderful weekend!
---
| |
---

Warm Regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO

clean_body: | | | | | | Dear Friend, In 2018, Mississippi passed a law restricting abortion procedures after the first 15 weeks of pregnancy. This law, which the Mississippi Center for Public Policy played a key role in preparing, has never been applied. A number of courts have ruled that the law violates women’s rights as established in Roe v. Wade in 1973. This week, however, the case – Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization – finally reached the Supreme Court, which must now decide if the 15-week restriction is indeed constitutional. It could be that the Supreme Court upholds the decisions by the lower courts, and nothing changes. But it might just be that we see a really quite subtle and important change in America’s political process that goes far beyond the issue of abortion. | | If the Supreme Court were to rule in favor of Mississippi’s law, women across America would continue to have less restricted access to abortion than women have in most European states. Claims that the Supreme Court ruling would ban abortion strike me as far-fetched. What Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization might just do is not just overturn Roe v. Wade as much as upend the assumption that it is for authorities at the federal level to decide what the law ought to be uniformly on this issue. If Mississippi was free to do things a little differently, might not Massachusetts or Maryland, or Michigan follow suit? And in what direction might they choose to diverge? And if states, not federal institutions, could do some things differently, why not others? The effect of taking power away from judges in DC to decide, and giving it to those elected in each state would be profound. Judicial activism has reduced a lot of America’s national politics to a process of trying to pack Courts and skew judicial nominations. If the Supreme Court rules that it should really be up to elected officials at the state level to decide more of the policy, politics might once again become an exercise in persuasion. I suspect America would end up with better public policy overall. Watch this case with interest. body:
[](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE1mxq93sbNTIt67N1gIeuSelhe43hv8C6fJTfEQkmViJQYJKHiICnOX-LI5Sfa2ttgMj-DmlkD2FlaxGj_UFhHUSCaQ6zb5OY3dm_hQ3kCM3HOCTWDOZmFJOBLmTaXVHUD7EOxfpH-1u0m7zVsGUsOJjF8lmi6d1g7gBRiHQYX0Ob7t_PKdsPiFHP0Q0EK-z_eAK6mgj30Fk_MRB2OBoUO0dj3X0sB-e6iNo-c-8Y4OedGfP9S-SjTuVhB9qNAiQiDP5swAx1mQx9NUz8vUPonIh_PX_gyrWMi5IzVWmExR0pBGLffPD7Tp1PTkRA4pFTQ%3D%3D) [**> > JOIN OUR TEAM <<
**](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE1mxq93sbNTIt67N1gIeuSelhe43hv8C6fJTfEQkmViJQYJKHiICnOX-LI5Sfa2ttgMj-DmlkD2FlaxGj_UFhHUSCaQ6zb5OY3dm_hQ3kCM36mDHfW1WAbkdI8jooNGrhTjYOEXQ0uT0A_yWpJsRNGdr-AvpJLH0MMfYJwpq2-oRcgA-bdBjIQO6Zw4FSOxcqXHTx50uYeIUxk4pzPK0NM12BimsOm2EViEXYfMr7udhtNWLQjxitGqxUFmNVLhg60HEgehzpJP1yHuc7-pWwaI2--9L-SiUxPc163568jbTsVRz910Rw8zhlz-svB9yuA%3D%3D) My friend, Our freedoms and our culture are under attack by the Left: high taxes, high regulation, Biden vaccine mandate, Walz lockdown of houses of worship and businesses during the early stages of the pandemic, growth of the Nanny State, and more. The most fundamental of those freedoms – **the freedom to protect one’s life** , the lives of loved ones, and one’s property – is regularly under assault by Leftist politicians, the mainstream media, and their enablers. **The Second Amendment is the great guarantor of that freedom** to protect ourselves and our families. Thanks to the conservative ruling of the U.S. Supreme Court in the _District of Columbia v. Heller_ case, the Second Amendment is held to protect the individual's right to keep and bear arms. But the Left continues to want to disarm ordinary people...even with the crime wave which has surged here in Minnesota since the summer of 2020. Remember their claim that only the police should carry guns, not private citizens? How does that even square with their more recent demands to defund the police?! If you or your neighbor isn’t allowed to keep a gun for self-defense, who is supposed to protect you from the criminals plaguing our communities? Mental health specialists and social workers who are supposed to replace sworn officers in the Progressives’ scheme of “reimagining public safety?" Don’t ask Leftists and liberals to be logical! When he previously served in Congress, Tim Walz was a pro-gun and seemingly centrist Democrat. He received an “A” rating from the National Rifle Association. Then he decided to run for statewide office and moved to the hard Left. Suddenly he transformed himself into an enemy of the Second Amendment (See “Not sticking to his guns: In race for governor, Walz embraces role as gun-control candidate,” [MinnPost](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE_YREZwaP6uKmt27SNgdqApee7M-r7rpg3XIH9ymjU-JNlWBFnNBfrh6Aoj_WkaWCLPWxlr8qdXB3t_H-TRqrxS26ZrTmbae7hMdeaZ-K9J4GbjbOEPRUzdVi0AzIeSW0KaavTymZv21f3d7rCsAymKcFARFKJimeJX-0QmD1xpgP60zgHBW02pNgYAeCgyZUd5AEc5jwEAxkWk_Qdy1H0JGQvr-7IwiD49Xd5GP1VDlWvyuTtVCk8x7XE55ciLDwRXJOCLwE6PxiwXidx-DCBRGZRApro9kjp9N8C5zdFJh7S6Pz6EYgdgtvL5nOJSrOOL7KCGJWONRcLpDlXe9wJV5YBPCJVYUYr6aKsuJzv0-1UpDXuVnzRkjbM9jhrl3u4x1SUEchRaodSS_wV9v44o%3D), 9/13/2018). **In the 2018 campaign for governor, he even bragged about now getting an “F” rating from the NRA** (Walz [Twitter](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE4cKCk0ms2ncxuyUiQvwGPwJQ_j_RqyGX5vyy0Sz1PYMijaD9YxPqfCAl1bAcjcTwyZiLvwukIa6amk9RuDLJfE0xtMoEXTzUDfbFQc2nzkPVn9Z_YhIz35CiseBQhH-HvRsEyimwsGF693tm_vsJ1fS8QMKGCFLip1mKHmaVmUE_S3sAvu8Rap9mhers8PVW4ZODD9qb67PRaZRlLnsL84AQtPsuOgtIdlBg6hqqIfJJjPlG1GsKvAp7nZrGQMH22pJN2v_DWKBh4ftn1OO1bjQ0PEP949WLeYNYHtukOfe), 8/2/2018). In so doing, Tim Walz committed the most brazen act of political treachery and personal hypocrisy one could imagine. Walz’s betrayal of gun owners and the NRA wasn’t just a political expediency to get himself elected in 2018. He brought a true believer’s zeal to his new-found embrace of gun control. He pushed gun control measures in the 2019 legislative session and hasn’t stopped. Since the Minnesota House had flipped to DFL control in the 2018 elections, we in the Minnesota Senate were the only obstacle to Governor Walz’s gun control plans in 2019. My colleagues and I stood strong because **we believe in the Constitution** , unlike our opponents. “Gazelka has blocked a pair of gun control bills from getting a hearing in a Senate committee,” reported the [_Pioneer Press_](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE9R3Yz3F_-HT1ULcbkosKLtA3-rTOxRYxfISPp5N_lhW3WyTMTGdxg8C8cZIINhZnSQD165P2jEE28nr-Er9BrhxrBO_jxPJCdFD8Su_NkUxMrAC3h7HwIfUF8sYklvxUMCC94sjcfAXk5efkT6cVFk5zERw2ZL9ZVZlGwotHQQAkYGfap7pmu-vSGKDI0YNvWgpChKczTQ0C5KmUPWrVWXi_CL6JbAHTsqOa9kIK88wYnsnRT4MJjqGUXcKlbOthp-3IHq0Bc_jvpYRRmeGcQ3g4DtW3RGEptjEtY5xJmZZtH4wFTLGtQRRrOTNaq5Z7DV0R8UmuliIx_G-8mQ5rD-DtGkK4gR9Mru9VYjPYKp62iQpXvrG95zkv8mNxBlpzQ%3D%3D) (8/5/2019). We stuck to our principles, though the Left were (unsurprisingly) unsparing in their nastiness. Even the governor’s wife got into the act. In an unprecedented break from past First Lady decorum, she infamously threatened political reprisals against my senate caucus members. As the [Star Tribune](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE4ki3lU6LqM4nI3COKnWuPj2E_XFONOYPF8-KY_tqvm2ugWu7inT2FnMXOeuAx2J54kz6Ss8mMOIQwDPIelGC4nahoJS3eFKclgoVchAx9BiWCutsxEk_NGBkGO_tX-XACF_0aWPKWjX2DjxS7u8AYEETOIKGRkoGGitUbJFdTxtg35uKb0sfQTbWI9-6h3jImovHQh5FtIhEdMh0zEblMpHMzi-Hch2LYqHe1RMbiNRcxpNjtGOe-mCWysNWst2_tW-rgJXpDla6ZpcEOVwT6NElcnw4CvY0mnLMxQX6kKKzrXrAkbFizNp7qU71B2ziSNY4Ex5tEPLr7dc_gBXP61XqbCNQDkqZoWYecfbTYD25rNpyhV5zkp9fGcO_7wuhLpH3yqSSKJTGoTJ2vKHinU%3D) reported (3/14/2019): _“If they do not put it up for a vote, there are seven senators sitting in seats where Tim Walz won — and we are coming [in 2020],” Gwen Walz said._ _But Senate Majority Leader Paul Gazelka, R-Nisswa, has promised to stand in the way of any new gun restrictions in his chamber._ And the Left did come for us in last year’s elections. But they failed to exploit what they thought was a winning issue: Their dream of using the gun control issue against us Republicans didn’t work. Even in a year when Joe Biden won Minnesota, I led the Senate Republican caucus to a resounding victory in preserving our majority. As a result, Republicans have continued to prevent Walz and the DFL from implementing their ruinous agenda on our state and its people, and Minnesota’s God-given and Constitutionally protected right to self-defense remains safe. The lesson of this story is that betrayal of core principals is not only morally wrong. It’s bad politics. It brings me no pleasure to have to point out that my former fellow state senator, Scott Jensen, did the same thing as Tim Walz on the same issue. Scott Jensen was elected in 2016 with the support of the NRA and gun owners in his safe, Republican district in Carver County. Then, Jensen inexplicably changed his position on the Second Amendment after one year in office. As the Minnesota Gun Owners Caucus (3/12/2018) [tweeted](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE5LizAHxQGcVpFlnTMsLgrkGj3kT_MexThU6vz5MIK2mtMA04ZEPzsBxII1eCiqNvwoO9_PlP6awXJS1aIYIdc5TWsDqmVPEui9FIVSFsGx7LyulLgr5vlOouYPpOVtlAgs59e5qFjHg0qomPI3vIPLcFIrptzHcZtjbqE1C66lIrLhR9kBJykXAqT-zP5biWhQHHdD5qTA5ys307AGvVCFeqEbwXDSY9-bs-zUyRVgPzcOD4CXhntbgJgWCK3iaYWQ3Cyzac865N3fCn-mwlFuof2ND8zgENu1p16jvOvwM) “MN GUN OWNERS BETRAYED BY SENATOR @drscottjensen” and “SCOTT JENSEN LIED TO GUN OWNERS.”
[](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE1mxq93sbNTIt67N1gIeuSelhe43hv8C6fJTfEQkmViJQYJKHiICnOX-LI5Sfa2ttgMj-DmlkD2FlaxGj_UFhHUSCaQ6zb5OY3dm_hQ3kCM3FUtfTBjNIk7T8bPzXf7jtMUa4KEqICQQEF5xfR4hg5q252qKAFr7QLpv1cQ3soENnZCViVoPPNy3Z47Vl5fMEht3HB6UHSQvXfq9A_1fq1pV5lpCqM48BMCU4AJz5qPqlsLjyR6oeDp3i7qkXJuDW8S3dPzO6TcNjosPSN-jUt7sEj3VNRbmuaStOFYUCz-JeiOKcOTw0_D4Wa5eSu80Gw%3D%3D) Jensen admitted that he flip-flopped on a major issue. “Absolutely I may have changed from what I wrote on the [2016 Gun Owners Caucus’s candidate survey] form two years ago.” ([Bring Me The News](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE5lOEcaRozlsvAS1d-fps26ZfTJFdTr-kTJ2dmqw4290LLyrzYQXewR5OBIXvCziM7RReiUD7KLwpIKcaB0DkR70Nj3rxF1AUg_s5knLTM0mXgCxv3mGu8UPQe9YabUd_dDdJ6Lmpkp_D5mOnQFihrRW57YjF-aEriNH-wTMM0jlbzN1iZE3c1_rQG4sLnzHE0TReQ9ea0CXTRUq10ZC8NrS1kfixnfJ-zkMfYkb4taaNA45-wjhfT4YqYRMRV3BJn_vJXseONWhOG3w0rCpnql1an9VzFjfYBDiHhagrhEmqmkH8lEDPF-bAojZzSKRwyEl92n72SnawiIEXzVu4sdUzKlTBAsEloREB76Doei9), 3/13/2018.) The Left had a field day with Jensen’s switch: “Senator with NRA tie joins push for new gun laws” blared [Minnesota Public Radio](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDEyiDBLqyGfkwmLW9w1l-qej4Hu2GoDeO5ffEXHjbCHwEy5U1j31vXjZXhAw5SiNIUhS5KcbTnvWmfqdeC9dhxbKXhMo-Q3-65Bha2zWBfSsr4jh2R13z3GTBHiCxAgx8esu_hESx4Ib1n89VIYEPR3jmfbMJadKc-a3NAMXhhG9LevH6ZZk0axzDw2rikYmZ1EYpo5Qqibeacfy-9zylNr-4jwpa4iXxSVfzajM6x0jAak9-S9DU7SZozm9lLp63pboj0u-dJ_2GJrR5-rmNkTxNkwIHRPSb_2gGqAN0LHnTFNjfNcRV2v0hqbusORsuRUmCbe_sha5i142zaWTcbus%3D) (3/12/2018), saying that Jensen “added his voice Monday to push for new gun restrictions in Minnesota.” [](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE1mxq93sbNTIt67N1gIeuSelhe43hv8C6fJTfEQkmViJQYJKHiICnOX-LI5Sfa2ttgMj-DmlkD2FlaxGj_UFhHUSCaQ6zb5OY3dm_hQ3kCM3B4mqTWvc63SbqDBfqJRAvolUFV23LKLw0zxVgfXzzEvFXSC_7Li-6iKKZpHgK0uw-pHxrnBwzMtSpY1KHZPpxbM_y98baqahErQuE4t71sDm9KBA1a1Fdo_sCbnLfDqXere7Jct8HBm2Vf3p0sZeCp7LhwAui-jLXcz8ppAiLBOaqp4HZ1rF0Wyo3O8jwOroF5YWjGAvjd9oovpfvjD1zQ%3D%3D) Jensen had become so committed to the anti-gun cause, he even said “I don’t think I’m going to lose my seat over this issue, but if I do, _que sera_.” After his betrayal of the Second Amendment, how can he ever be trusted again? Well, Scott Jensen apparently thinks all of our memories are short. Now that he is running for governor, he’s flip-flopped again! Jensen’s campaign [website](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE8iCz3WQ-iWkWRoYFNWbOYt03qgBFDsfHOZsJ28TKujXKHoNy6uQ5q3sgRc5hcVOU8G8qpZ9-58NINfwTKQtbKc7LylQB1ruiPZLnJ-3Ib-GphRZ1DodUMt8s_JrEvms8oAHyiL_zs6WZ601A-izNA0woWg9zgm6XrCKlC9LOYFMqq73Yco4CtPG8lP_4zwawnLfYosm4C1o2gvTNschTgWImzJYUN6SeelxUrUe49V9yTS0yUspv-xtWCvN81MX9g%3D%3D) states “protecting the Second Amendment is a top priority of the Dr. Scott Jensen administration.” [](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE1mxq93sbNTIt67N1gIeuSelhe43hv8C6fJTfEQkmViJQYJKHiICnOX-LI5Sfa2ttgMj-DmlkD2FlaxGj_UFhHUSCaQ6zb5OY3dm_hQ3kCM3URDka1_U3sgGiZovm9A2IPQo3X2hqtyWd3yOW33uUnug53Nyr7w711hvg14e_n-avUMhSmM4Q7EFnFI61Xwi9hSOjZMoDh2oBnK3z05SLD-0KDuFpkOx6ePbSVtcl9wEuUBoGh9YRLeUyPJuCGoNnBb7vg07Z1wovLqbcczt6X6sxGW5jbBCRlA8fKx0Tosn7rz-6VMS19mcKP4BIRQOJA%3D%3D) He goes even further by making all sorts of grandiose pro-gun statements: He says he “would expand gun rights as governor.” He says he “would be a staunch defender of gun rights.” “If universal background checks were passed (in a hypothetical Democrat-controlled legislature), would you sign it?” Dr. Scott Jensen: “No I would not.” Even though he broke ranks in 2018 to sponsor universal background check legislation! It seems to me that Scott Jensen is insulting the intelligence of every Republican activist by making these absurd claims. Jensen is trying to camouflage his betrayal of the Second Amendment, his embracing an anti-Constitutional position, his active involvement in sponsoring gun control legislation, his having given aid and comfort to Tim Walz and the DFL on this issue, his treachery towards fellow Republican caucus members who were getting enormous political heat from the Left for their principled opposition, and his claimed re-conversion to a pro-gun stance. Scott Jensen’s contortions are enough to make anyone’s head spin! As I said previously, **I don’t like having to write this**. As a party leader and especially during my five years as Senate Majority Leader, I have always believed in observing Ronald Reagan’s Eleventh Commandment: “Thou shalt not criticize a fellow Republican.” But Scott Jensen has an extensive record of deviating from conservative principals and Republican policies. It is a deeply troubling record which you deserve to know and one which, frankly, suggests that his commitment to Republican principles is highly dubious. My record, on the other hand, is clear and beyond reproach in supporting the Second Amendment and understanding the Founders’ original intent of it as a guarantee of individual citizens’ right to keep and bear arms. It has been 100% consistent. And it was tested repeatedly – and proven to be rock solid – as Senate Majority Leader, under both intense DFL pressure from outside and the attempted but failed efforts to undermine me from within the caucus by then-Senator Jensen. If I earn your endorsement, you can count on me to be a governor who respects and upholds the Constitution and won’t abandon conservative principles and common sense. The same cannot be said for Scott Jensen. **Protecting the Constitution and the people’s inherent rights is not a political game.** It’s not a rhetorical exercise. It has real implications for ordinary Minnesotans who are trying to live their lives in the real world. In the hours and days immediately following the burning of Minneapolis’s Third Precinct and Lake Street, ordinary citizens – business owners, their friends and neighbors – were on the sidewalks and rooftops in south Minneapolis openly carrying firearms as a spontaneous, lawful, respectful and peaceable show of force to defend businesses and buildings from the arsonists and looters. It was a rare but large-scale demonstration of the importance of the Second Amendment. Private citizens were doing what Tim Walz and Jacob Frey refused to do. This simple fact during those frightening days in 2020 is all the validation needed for our party platform’s support for the Second Amendment. In my previous Senate leadership role, I led the charge against anti-gunowner legislation. I gave unwavering support to my staunch colleague Sen. Warren Limmer in resisting gun control proposals in his committee and in conference committees. The NRA’s Institute for Legislative Action [wrote](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=3fcb452ec5b84111b419c69f3fa50201&_m=8ac828f31b8f41468fbd83fee2ebc4b1&_e=vglvLugstvSpBUWP1LgDE8rDlmj2qnr5q10ueQuIvZ-Lc5_BUnB596YwtOJh5-4iByN7h0pD9X5AdtKrffF6TKbTI581YlhYzFY503wcEIu8dDYgyAud-LmI9n9efDo0ZX3az6CfY2o5kJrIQXZJCt-rc1wTQlJbR44pfWLDjVX8HjZ8ImDN3N9IyJNBrz0PrPjnHi-NoRmZxMehUFrs9W3vf6lFWEbGvAj1HscGOkKRZsSDydzDHfNRMPOWYtUbkHqLEg56Qr9H07G5HM1i-dSYA3HfmUiaGjn-jF8Rpu0vJ_TPKArJnKhHlbhrg8YkU9kaomZJdceQqYR5I8X0MkD1ZsPh9659BVxLNciPe4o%3D) just last summer at the conclusion of the Legislature’s special session: _Special thanks to Senate Majority Leader Paul Gazelka for leading the way over the last five years in defeating all gun control measures, including gun confiscation “red flag” laws and “universal” background checks. _ Unlike flip-floppers like Tim Walz and Scott Jensen, I will remain a faithful friend of the Second Amendment as governor just as I have as a state legislator. I will defend the rights of the hundreds of thousands of ordinary, law-abiding Minnesotans who protect themselves by owning a firearm or may find themselves needing to do so one day. Thank you! clean_body: My friend, Our freedoms and our culture are under attack by the Left: high taxes, high regulation, Biden vaccine mandate, Walz lockdown of houses of worship and businesses during the early stages of the pandemic, growth of the Nanny State, and more. The most fundamental of those freedoms – the freedom to protect one’s life , the lives of loved ones, and one’s property – is regularly under assault by Leftist politicians, the mainstream media, and their enablers. The Second Amendment is the great guarantor of that freedom to protect ourselves and our families. Thanks to the conservative ruling of the U.S. Supreme Court in the District of Columbia v. Heller case, the Second Amendment is held to protect the individual's right to keep and bear arms. But the Left continues to want to disarm ordinary people...even with the crime wave which has surged here in Minnesota since the summer of 2020. Remember their claim that only the police should carry guns, not private citizens? How does that even square with their more recent demands to defund the police?! If you or your neighbor isn’t allowed to keep a gun for self-defense, who is supposed to protect you from the criminals plaguing our communities? Mental health specialists and social workers who are supposed to replace sworn officers in the Progressives’ scheme of “reimagining public safety?" Don’t ask Leftists and liberals to be logical! When he previously served in Congress, Tim Walz was a pro-gun and seemingly centrist Democrat. He received an “A” rating from the National Rifle Association. Then he decided to run for statewide office and moved to the hard Left. Suddenly he transformed himself into an enemy of the Second Amendment (See “Not sticking to his guns: In race for governor, Walz embraces role as gun-control candidate,” , 9/13/2018). In the 2018 campaign for governor, he even bragged about now getting an “F” rating from the NRA (Walz , 8/2/2018). In so doing, Tim Walz committed the most brazen act of political treachery and personal hypocrisy one could imagine. Walz’s betrayal of gun owners and the NRA wasn’t just a political expediency to get himself elected in 2018. He brought a true believer’s zeal to his new-found embrace of gun control. He pushed gun control measures in the 2019 legislative session and hasn’t stopped. Since the Minnesota House had flipped to DFL control in the 2018 elections, we in the Minnesota Senate were the only obstacle to Governor Walz’s gun control plans in 2019. My colleagues and I stood strong because we believe in the Constitution , unlike our opponents. “Gazelka has blocked a pair of gun control bills from getting a hearing in a Senate committee,” reported the (8/5/2019). We stuck to our principles, though the Left were (unsurprisingly) unsparing in their nastiness. Even the governor’s wife got into the act. In an unprecedented break from past First Lady decorum, she infamously threatened political reprisals against my senate caucus members. As the reported (3/14/2019): “If they do not put it up for a vote, there are seven senators sitting in seats where Tim Walz won — and we are coming [in 2020],” Gwen Walz said. But Senate Majority Leader Paul Gazelka, R-Nisswa, has promised to stand in the way of any new gun restrictions in his chamber. And the Left did come for us in last year’s elections. But they failed to exploit what they thought was a winning issue: Their dream of using the gun control issue against us Republicans didn’t work. Even in a year when Joe Biden won Minnesota, I led the Senate Republican caucus to a resounding victory in preserving our majority. As a result, Republicans have continued to prevent Walz and the DFL from implementing their ruinous agenda on our state and its people, and Minnesota’s God-given and Constitutionally protected right to self-defense remains safe. The lesson of this story is that betrayal of core principals is not only morally wrong. It’s bad politics. It brings me no pleasure to have to point out that my former fellow state senator, Scott Jensen, did the same thing as Tim Walz on the same issue. Scott Jensen was elected in 2016 with the support of the NRA and gun owners in his safe, Republican district in Carver County. Then, Jensen inexplicably changed his position on the Second Amendment after one year in office. As the Minnesota Gun Owners Caucus (3/12/2018) “MN GUN OWNERS BETRAYED BY SENATOR @drscottjensen” and “SCOTT JENSEN LIED TO GUN OWNERS.” Jensen admitted that he flip-flopped on a major issue. “Absolutely I may have changed from what I wrote on the [2016 Gun Owners Caucus’s candidate survey] form two years ago.” (, 3/13/2018.) The Left had a field day with Jensen’s switch: “Senator with NRA tie joins push for new gun laws” blared (3/12/2018), saying that Jensen “added his voice Monday to push for new gun restrictions in Minnesota.” Jensen had become so committed to the anti-gun cause, he even said “I don’t think I’m going to lose my seat over this issue, but if I do, que sera.” After his betrayal of the Second Amendment, how can he ever be trusted again? Well, Scott Jensen apparently thinks all of our memories are short. Now that he is running for governor, he’s flip-flopped again! Jensen’s campaign states “protecting the Second Amendment is a top priority of the Dr. Scott Jensen administration.” He goes even further by making all sorts of grandiose pro-gun statements: He says he “would expand gun rights as governor.” He says he “would be a staunch defender of gun rights.” “If universal background checks were passed (in a hypothetical Democrat-controlled legislature), would you sign it?” Dr. Scott Jensen: “No I would not.” Even though he broke ranks in 2018 to sponsor universal background check legislation! It seems to me that Scott Jensen is insulting the intelligence of every Republican activist by making these absurd claims. Jensen is trying to camouflage his betrayal of the Second Amendment, his embracing an anti-Constitutional position, his active involvement in sponsoring gun control legislation, his having given aid and comfort to Tim Walz and the DFL on this issue, his treachery towards fellow Republican caucus members who were getting enormous political heat from the Left for their principled opposition, and his claimed re-conversion to a pro-gun stance. Scott Jensen’s contortions are enough to make anyone’s head spin! As I said previously, I don’t like having to write this. As a party leader and especially during my five years as Senate Majority Leader, I have always believed in observing Ronald Reagan’s Eleventh Commandment: “Thou shalt not criticize a fellow Republican.” But Scott Jensen has an extensive record of deviating from conservative principals and Republican policies. It is a deeply troubling record which you deserve to know and one which, frankly, suggests that his commitment to Republican principles is highly dubious. My record, on the other hand, is clear and beyond reproach in supporting the Second Amendment and understanding the Founders’ original intent of it as a guarantee of individual citizens’ right to keep and bear arms. It has been 100% consistent. And it was tested repeatedly – and proven to be rock solid – as Senate Majority Leader, under both intense DFL pressure from outside and the attempted but failed efforts to undermine me from within the caucus by then-Senator Jensen. If I earn your endorsement, you can count on me to be a governor who respects and upholds the Constitution and won’t abandon conservative principles and common sense. The same cannot be said for Scott Jensen. Protecting the Constitution and the people’s inherent rights is not a political game. It’s not a rhetorical exercise. It has real implications for ordinary Minnesotans who are trying to live their lives in the real world. In the hours and days immediately following the burning of Minneapolis’s Third Precinct and Lake Street, ordinary citizens – business owners, their friends and neighbors – were on the sidewalks and rooftops in south Minneapolis openly carrying firearms as a spontaneous, lawful, respectful and peaceable show of force to defend businesses and buildings from the arsonists and looters. It was a rare but large-scale demonstration of the importance of the Second Amendment. Private citizens were doing what Tim Walz and Jacob Frey refused to do. This simple fact during those frightening days in 2020 is all the validation needed for our party platform’s support for the Second Amendment. In my previous Senate leadership role, I led the charge against anti-gunowner legislation. I gave unwavering support to my staunch colleague Sen. Warren Limmer in resisting gun control proposals in his committee and in conference committees. The NRA’s Institute for Legislative Action just last summer at the conclusion of the Legislature’s special session: Special thanks to Senate Majority Leader Paul Gazelka for leading the way over the last five years in defeating all gun control measures, including gun confiscation “red flag” laws and “universal” background checks. Unlike flip-floppers like Tim Walz and Scott Jensen, I will remain a faithful friend of the Second Amendment as governor just as I have as a state legislator. I will defend the rights of the hundreds of thousands of ordinary, law-abiding Minnesotans who protect themselves by owning a firearm or may find themselves needing to do so one day. Thank you! Paul Gazelka | | | | ---|---|--- Paid for and Provided by Gazelka for Governor P.O. Box 175 Little Falls, MN 56345

I grew up in a small working class town in California. My dad was a teacher, and my mom was a secretary. They worked hard, but always made time for family.

At first, my parents couldn't afford to send me to university -- so I started at community college instead. Like many students, I paid my way through school by working part time.

I wanted to be a teacher to give back to my community. After 20 years, it's clear that opportunities to thrive for many families are being cut short by politicians like Kevin McCarthy. He's represented us for nearly 15 years with NOTHING to show for it except trying to advance his own career.

**I hear stories from my kiddos and their parents about the challenges they face -- while they work harder, wages stagnate and the ultra-wealthy are getting richer and richer.**

Heck, my family has had our fair share of struggles, but like many families here in the Central Valley, you just learn to deal. It's unacceptable that our current representative is doing so much to make things actively worse for our community. That's just not how we do things around here.

I'm stepping up to run for Congress because I can't take Kevin McCarthy's blatant disregard for our community any longer, and I'm asking you to join me:

**[We're coming up $9,301 short of our weekly goal to DEFEAT Kevin McCarthy. That means we need to see A TON of supporters chipping in a grassroots donation before 11:59 pm tonight. clean_body: (about me) | Friend, = | = I grew up in a small working class town in California. My dad was a teacher, and my mom was a secretary. They worked hard, but always made time for family. At first, my parents couldn't afford to send me to university -- so I started at community college instead. Like many students, I paid my way through school by working part time. I wanted to be a teacher to give back to my community. After 20 years, it's clear that opportunities to thrive for many families are being cut short by politicians like Kevin McCarthy. He's represented us for nearly 15 years with NOTHING to show for it except trying to advance his own career. I hear stories from my kiddos and their parents about the challenges they face -- while they work harder, wages stagnate and the ultra-wealthy are getting richer and richer. Heck, my family has had our fair share of struggles, but like many families here in the Central Valley, you just learn to deal. It's unacceptable that our current representative is doing so much to make things actively worse for our community. That's just not how we do things around here. I'm stepping up to run for Congress because I can't take Kevin McCarthy's blatant disregard for our community any longer, and I'm asking you to join me: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thanks in advance, Marisa Wood Teacher for Congress = | = Marisa Wood is a public school teacher and Democrat running a historic campaign to unseat GOP Leader Kevin McCarthy in California's 23rd District. 2022 is our best shot in decades to finally defeat McCarthy. Consider supporting Marisa's campaign by = If you'd prefer to send a check, our address is: Marisa Wood for Congress, PO Box 12103, Bakersfield, CA 93389. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Marisa Wood for Congress PO Box 12103 Bakersfield, CA 93389 United States

name: Mondaire Jones
subject: Thank you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_
body:
#

Friend,
I ran for Congress because the world is on fire and we need fighters at the policy-making table.
We need folks who understand and feel the urgency to address the daily concerns that Americans face as minimum wage remains stagnant and the costs of housing, higher education, and child care rise.
I grew up with a single mom who worked multiple jobs to make ends meet. We were lucky to have my grandparents there to help — but I often still had to accompany my grandmother to work because day care was too expensive.
The policies that I’m fighting for in Congress are personal. It's shameful that, in the richest country in the world, millions of kids have no choice but to go to work with their guardians.
**Thank you for your unwavering support this year.**
Month after month, you’ve shown up and helped us hit our fundraising goals — so I want to acknowledge your role in ensuring I have the resources to get re-elected in 2022.
Together, we’ve shown that it’s possible to run a campaign fueled entirely by grassroots donors who are invested in seeing a new generation of working-class champions legislating in Congress.
We’ve come a long way, but we still have plenty of tough challenges ahead as we fight for policies that lighten the burden on American families and protect our democracy from the GOP and their mission to strip millions of their fundamental rights.
Let’s finish the year off strong and keep up the fight in December.
Onward,
Mondaire

body:

---
Peter, Three years ago, the North Carolina state legislature passed a law requiring voters to present a form of I.D. before casting their ballots. **This was a reasonable measure to ensure our elections are safe and secure. But Democrat Attorney General Josh Stein has put his liberal politics above the will of the people of North Carolina**.
---
[ **SUPPORT VOTER ID NOW**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5dffe9cf8697fc0a3a89ac0c84d83476/35903/9f44c26ac92c3616/46647159b9daa209c3d6b838967d376c/35903)

---
Now, Republican state legislators are before the U.S. Supreme Court requesting a ‘motion to intervene’ over the state’s lawsuits requiring voter ID. If Republican legislators win, it allows them to join the legal proceedings happening in the lower courts – helping to give the people of North Carolina a voice in the legal battle to uphold NC's crucial voter ID law. Peter, let me make my position on this clear – I support voter ID 100%, and I’ll fight vigorously to secure our elections in the U.S. Senate. _**Because it should be easier to vote and harder to cheat**_. **[So please, click HERE to rush an urgent donation to help me win this race and lead the charge for voter integrity in Washington.>>](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5dffe9cf8697fc0a3a89ac0c84d83476/35903/9f44c26ac92c3616/7d9ce4e77b43905bc3d6b838967d376c/35903)** We MUST take a stand against the left and safeguard our elections in 2022, 2024, and beyond. Unlike our MAGA opponent Devin Nunes, we don’t bow down to big corporations or ultra-rich campaign donors! Do you support a movement that believes in making Americans and their interests the priority?
